introduction: This presentation provides insights into the IT & Security operations at Lee's Market in 2024, focusing on hardware management. It particularly highlights the update and replacement of employee computers.

subsections.heading: Overview of IT Assets Management
subsections.content:
• Lee's Market's IT Assets Management policy was updated in 2023 to align with the new industry standards
• The IT department utilizes IT Asset Management (ITAM) software to track and manage assets across all locations
• Regular audits are conducted to ensure all assets are accounted for and are in good working condition
• IT Asset Management plays a crucial role in ensuring business continuity and mitigating risks
• In 2024, the IT department rolled out a new system for better tracking of software licenses
• The IT department has implemented a robust disposal process for obsolete or end-of-life IT assets
subsections.justification: This slide talks about IT assets management in general, not about the specific number of computers replaced in 2024.

subsections.heading: Employee Computer Replacement Statistics
subsections.content:
• The IT department has a protocol to replace computers every 3-5 years depending on the usage and technical specifications
• The 2023 computer replacement program saw a 15% increase from the previous year
• The IT department has a set budget for computer replacements which is reviewed annually
• The cost of computer replacements has decreased by 10% from 2020 to 2024 due to better procurement strategies
• The number of computers replaced in 2024 was slightly higher than the average of the last five years
subsections.justification: This slide gives an overview of computer replacement statistics but does not provide the specific number for 2024.

subsections.heading: Impact on Operational Efficiency
subsections.content:
• Regular computer replacements ensure that employees have the tools they need to perform their jobs efficiently
• The IT department tracks downtime related to computer issues and has seen a decrease in the past two years
• The implementation of new technology has contributed to a 10% increase in operational efficiency in 2024
• The IT department's rapid response to hardware issues minimizes disruptions in store operations
• Employee satisfaction with IT support increased by 8% in 2024, according to the annual employee survey
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the impact of IT operations on efficiency, not the specific number of computers replaced by Jason Wong's team in 2024.

conclusion: The proactive approach to computer replacements has had a significant positive impact on operational efficiency. Moving forward, we recommend continued investment in IT infrastructure to maintain this momentum.
